meta.source: Microsoft Learn (learn.microsoft.com/sharepoint, /microsoft-365) — admin-center paths, setting labels, and Set-SPOTenant equivalents grounded via the Microsoft Learn MCP on 2026-07-03. Shares mechanisms with the Tier1 sharepoint-onedrive guidance; Tier0 target values applied.
generalNotes: SharePoint/OneDrive settings live in the SharePoint admin center (Policies > Access control / Sharing, and Settings > OneDrive). Most have a Set-SPOTenant PowerShell equivalent (SharePoint Online Management Shell / PnP).
Several of these are verify-only baseline rows (Mac sync enabled, no sync exclusions, 1 TB quota) — the intent is to confirm and record the current state, not necessarily change it.

The Tier0 'OneDrive retention' and 'Deleted user data retention' rows are the SAME underlying setting (deleted user's OneDrive retention = 60 days) surfaced as two Inforcer objects — both should read 60.

requiredSettings.label: Requirement
requiredSettings.value: Relies on Conditional Access / Entra ID P1 (included with Business Premium); tenant-wide, browser sessions only

steps: SharePoint admin center > Policies > Access control > Idle session sign-out.
Turn on 'Sign out inactive users automatically'; set sign-out = 60 minutes and warning = 5 minutes.
Save. PowerShell equivalent: Set-SPOBrowserIdleSignOut -Enabled $true -WarnAfter (New-TimeSpan -Minutes 55) -SignOutAfter (New-TimeSpan -Minutes 60).
Verify: sign in to OneDrive/SharePoint in a browser, idle >55 min, confirm the warning then sign-out.

Note (MEDIUM impact): applies org-wide to SharePoint/OneDrive in the browser only (not desktop Office apps) and can't be scoped to specific users. Users on managed devices with SSO or 'Stay signed in' may not be affected; browser users who step away >1h can lose unsaved in-browser edits. For per-user targeting use Conditional Access sign-in frequency.

requiredSettings.label: Days to retain a deleted user's OneDrive
Relationship
requiredSettings.value: Same underlying setting as the 'Deleted user data retention' row — both must read 60

steps: SharePoint admin center > Settings > OneDrive - Retention.
Set 'Days to retain files in a deleted user's OneDrive' = 60.
Save. PowerShell equivalent: Set-SPOTenant -OrphanedPersonalSitesRetentionPeriod 60.
Verify: Get-SPOTenant | Select OrphanedPersonalSitesRetentionPeriod — expect 60.

Note (LOW impact): invisible to active users; this is the offboarding retention window. It is the same tenant setting as 'Deleted user data retention' (two Inforcer objects, one value) — set it once and both verify.

requiredSettings.label: Days to retain a deleted user's OneDrive
requiredSettings.value: 60 — during this window designated admins can access/transfer the content; after 60 days the OneDrive is permanently deleted

steps: SharePoint admin center > Settings > OneDrive - Retention (same page/setting as the 'OneDrive retention' row).
Confirm 'Days to retain files in a deleted user's OneDrive' = 60. PowerShell equivalent: Set-SPOTenant -OrphanedPersonalSitesRetentionPeriod 60.
Verify: Get-SPOTenant | Select OrphanedPersonalSitesRetentionPeriod — expect 60. The clock starts when the account is deleted from Entra ID.
Note (LOW impact): offboarding-only. Ensure offboarding runbooks retrieve or reassign a departed user's OneDrive within the 60-day window before permanent deletion.
SharePoint admin center > Settings > OneDrive - Storage limit.
Set the default storage to 1024 GB (1 TB) for all licensed users.
Save. PowerShell equivalent: Set-SPOTenant -OneDriveStorageQuota 1048576 (to re-apply to an existing user: Set-SPOSite -Identity <OneDrive URL> -StorageQuota 1048576).
Verify: Get-SPOTenant | Select OneDriveStorageQuota — expect 1048576.
Note (LOW impact): standard Business Premium/E3/E5 allocation — no user impact for typical use; raise per-user for large-data users. A value above the license cap is rounded down to that cap.

requiredSettings.label: OneDrive sync app for Mac
requiredSettings.value: Enabled (IsMacSyncAppEnabled = true) — Mac users can sync normally

steps: SharePoint admin center > Settings > OneDrive - Sync.
Confirm the OneDrive sync app is allowed for Mac devices (no Mac restriction).
Verify: Get-SPOTenant | Select IsMacSyncAppEnabled — expect True.

Note (LOW impact / verify-only): this is an enabler, not a restriction. To restrict OneDrive sync to managed/Intune-enrolled Macs only, use a separate Conditional Access policy targeting the OneDrive sync app rather than changing this toggle.

requiredSettings.label: File types excluded from OneDrive sync
requiredSettings.value: None (ExcludedFileExtensionsForSyncClient is empty) — all file types sync normally

steps: SharePoint admin center > Settings > OneDrive - Sync > 'Block upload of specific file types'.
Confirm no file extensions are listed (baseline state = no exclusions).
Verify: Get-SPOTenant | Select ExcludedFileExtensionsForSyncClient — expect empty.
Note (LOW impact / verify-only): to block types later (e.g. pst, iso, exe), Set-SPOTenant -ExcludedFileExtensionsForSyncClient 'pst','iso','exe'. Record the current empty state.

requiredSettings.label: Guest user access restrictions (directory)
requiredSettings.value: Most restrictive — 'restricted to properties and memberships of their own directory objects' (GuestUserRoleID = 10dae51f-b6af-4016-8d66-8c2a99b929b3, Restricted Guest User)

requiredSettings.label: Same control as
requiredSettings.value: The Entra 'Guest user access restrictions' row (Phase 3) — this is the directory-level guest permission level, surfaced from the M365 admin side

requiredSettings.label: Related M365 Groups guest toggles
requiredSettings.value: M365 Admin Center > Settings > Org settings > Services > Microsoft 365 Groups: 'Let group owners add people outside your organization…' and 'Let guest group members access group content'

steps: Authoritative control (GuestUserRoleID): Entra ID > Users > User settings > Guest user access restrictions — set to 'Guest user access is restricted to properties and memberships of their own directory objects (most restrictive)'. Save.

At the row's stated path — M365 Admin Center > Settings > Org settings > Services > Microsoft 365 Groups — review the two guest toggles ('let owners add external people', 'let guest members access group content') and set them to match the client's collaboration posture.
Verify: PowerShell (Microsoft.Graph) — (Get-MgPolicyAuthorizationPolicy).GuestUserRoleId should be 10dae51f-b6af-4016-8d66-8c2a99b929b3.

Note (LOW impact): guests get minimal directory visibility (can't enumerate users/groups); no impact on internal users. This intentionally mirrors the Entra External Identities guest restriction — configure once at the directory level and both rows verify.
